verdict_reasoning: KLK2 is a secreted serine protease with no transmembrane domain or known intrinsic membrane-anchoring mechanism. Baseline expression in normal prostate is cytoplasmic/intracellular. However, recent systematic studies (2025) confirm robust surface localization on prostate cancer cells (by FACS, IHC, and multiplex IF), with demonstrated targeting by bispecific T-cell engagers, radioconjugates, and CAR-T therapies. This represents a documented cancer-cell-state-induced ecto-form of a nominally intracellular/secreted protein. The protein body reaches the outer leaflet only in the transformed prostate cancer cell context, qualifying as contextual cell_state_induced.
